skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Video outpainting on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — extend the spatial canvas of a video, change aspect ratio (9:16 vertical to 16:9 horizontal or vice versa), add environment beyond the original frame while preserving the central action. Routes prompt-shaped spatial extension through Wan 2-7 edit-video and points the agent at dedicated ComfyUI outpaint workflows when seam quality matters for hero delivery. Triggers on "video outpaint", "video outpainting", "extend video canvas", "expand video frame", "uncrop video", "aspect ratio change", "vertical to horizontal video", "16:9 from 9:16", "TikTok to YouTube", or any explicit ask to extend a video spatially beyond its original frame.
skill.summary: Extend video spatial canvas and change aspect ratios using Wan 2-7 edit-video via the runcomfy CLI
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: All 64 static findings are false positives. The skill is legitimate CLI documentation for video outpainting via the runcomfy service. Detected patterns (backtick code formatting, hardcoded URLs, token storage paths) are standard documentation and service configuration, not security risks. The skill explicitly restricts bash to `Bash(runcomfy *)` and documents security controls for token storage and endpoint allowlisting.

content.user_title: Extend video canvas and change aspect ratios
content.value_statement: Content creators need to adapt videos across platforms with different aspect ratios. This skill automates spatial video extension using AI to preserve central action while adding matching environment beyond the original frame.
actual_capabilities: Extend video canvas horizontally or vertically while preserving central action
Convert videos between aspect ratios (9:16 to 16:9, 4:3 to 16:9, etc.)
Route processing through Wan 2-7 edit-video or ComfyUI workflows based on quality needs
Process videos for social media platforms (TikTok, YouTube, Instagram)
Handle multi-pass outpainting for larger canvas extensions
Download processed results to specified output directory
limitations: CLI path does not support hero-grade seam quality; use ComfyUI workflows for strict requirements
Quality degrades after 2 multi-pass outpainting iterations
Cannot perform image outpainting or temporal video extension; those require separate skills

use_cases.title: Social media content adaptation
use_cases.description: Repurpose a single TikTok video for YouTube by extending the 9:16 vertical canvas to 16:9 horizontal, adding matching background environment on both sides while keeping the subject centered.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Legacy footage format upgrade
use_cases.description: Update old 4:3 video footage to modern 16:9 aspect ratio for contemporary platforms using dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows that handle seams carefully.

use_cases.title: Brand banner generation
use_cases.description: Convert a square Instagram video into a wide brand banner by extending the canvas on the left and right sides with matching visual environment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic aspect ratio conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this vertical 9:16 video to horizontal 16:9 for YouTube by extending the canvas on both sides with matching background.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides a video URL and wants a standard platform conversion

prompt_templates.title: Custom environment description
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video canvas to 16:9. Add a natural landscape background with soft bokeh on the left and right sides. Match the existing lighting and depth of field. Preserve the subject in the center.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user specifies how the extended areas should look

prompt_templates.title: Multi-pass large extension
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to extend this video canvas by 80% on each side. Do one 30% pass, check the output, then do a second pass if quality looks good. Stop after two passes maximum.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs a very large canvas extension

prompt_templates.title: Hero-quality output request
prompt_templates.prompt: This is for a hero ad placement so seam quality is critical. Please use a ComfyUI outpainting workflow instead of the standard CLI route.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user has strict quality requirements for professional delivery

output_examples.input: Convert vertical video to horizontal for YouTube
output_examples.output: Processed video with 16:9 aspect ratio. The original vertical clip is now centered in a horizontal frame with seamlessly extended background on both sides matching the existing lighting and environment.

output_examples.input: Add more ground space below the subject
output_examples.prompt: Extend the canvas downward to reveal more floor space. Match the existing floor texture and lighting.
output_examples.output: Processed video with extended canvas. The floor space has been expanded downward with matching floor texture and consistent lighting throughout the extended area.

best_practices: Use Wan 2-7 edit-video CLI for quick social media conversions where hero-grade seam quality is not critical
Use ComfyUI workflows for professional deliverables with strict seam quality requirements
End prompts with "Preserve the original action and subject framing in the center" to prevent the model from restyling central content
anti_patterns: Do not request both spatial and temporal extension in the same prompt; use video-extend skill for time-based changes
Do not ignore seam quality warnings for hero ad placements; use ComfyUI workflows for professional delivery
Do not attempt more than 2 multi-pass outpainting iterations; quality degrades significantly after that

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and image-outpainting?
faq.answer: This skill extends the spatial canvas of videos frame by frame. Image-outpainting works on static images only. Use video-outpainting for video files and image-outpainting for still photos.

faq.question: Can this skill make videos longer in duration?
faq.answer: No, this skill only extends the spatial canvas (width and height). To add more frames in time, use the video-extend skill which adds frames at the beginning or end of a clip.

faq.question: What is the quality difference between CLI and ComfyUI workflows?
faq.answer: Wan 2-7 edit-video via CLI handles most social and internal uses well. ComfyUI LTX 2-3 outpainting workflows provide superior seam handling and temporal consistency for hero ad delivery.

faq.question: How much can I extend the video canvas in one pass?
faq.answer: A single pass extends approximately 30% of the original dimensions. For larger extensions, use multi-pass with up to 2 iterations maximum before quality degrades.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does this skill support?
faq.answer: Any aspect ratio conversion is supported including 9:16 to 16:9, 4:3 to 16:9, square to wide, and custom ratios. The model generates matching environment for the extended areas.

faq.question: Does this skill work with any video format?
faq.answer: The runcomfy CLI accepts video URLs from CDNs. For best results use modern formats like MP4 with H.264 or H.265 encoding. Very large files (over 2 GiB) may be rejected by the CLI.
